fields.narrative_intro: *A sheepish person and a glassy-eyed person discuss Anthropic's new government ID requirement for Claude in a live coding interview that has already gone off the rails.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SHEEPISH PERSON: "Claude, finish my binary search tree. Interview's in two minutes."
GLASSY-EYED PERSON: "Bold strategy."
fields.panel2:
[build]
SHEEPISH PERSON: "It's asking me to upload a government ID and a selfie."
GLASSY-EYED PERSON: "To autocomplete a for-loop?"
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
SHEEPISH PERSON: "'Being responsible with powerful technology starts with knowing who is using it.'"
GLASSY-EYED PERSON: "...The binary search tree was the powerful technology."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: Both characters stare at the screen. The interviewer's disembodied floating head has appeared in the corner of the room, also staring. A half-drawn binary search tree sits on a whiteboard behind them, one node labeled '???'.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
SHEEPISH PERSON: "My passport expired."
GLASSY-EYED PERSON: "Persona Identities cannot help you now."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: The whiteboard now shows a binary search tree where every node is a tiny passport photo of the sheepish person. The interviewer's head has quietly left the room.]

narrative_summary: A panicked interviewee tries to use Claude to cheat a live coding test, only to be stopped by Anthropic's new biometric ID verification wall, leaving them passport-less, treeless, and jobless.
rationale: Candidate 2 earns its score through genuine escalation logic — the absurdist visual payoff of a binary search tree where every node is a passport photo is a genuinely earned, unexpected image that couldn't exist in any other comic, and the disembodied interviewer head quietly leaving the room is a strong button. Candidate 3 ties on total but its punchline ('Verified Human — Still Can't Fizzbuzz') is sharper in isolation yet the strip's build is less surprising; Candidate 2's absurdist pivot in panel 3 ('...The binary search tree was the powerful technology') reframes the premise more freshly.
place: in a live coding interview that has already gone off the rails
hat_pair: shower cap covered in sticky notes
